item.title: TypeError: unhashable type: Python 中的 'dict' | TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict' in Python
item.description:
你有没有遇到过这样的错误信息：“TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict' in Python”？ 如果是，那么您并不孤单。 当您尝试将字典用作另一个字典或集合中的键时，会发生此错误。 在这篇博文中，我们将详细探讨此错误消息，了解其含义，并研究解决方法。 那么，让我们开始吧。

错误信息解释

当您在 Python 中声明字典时，它会创建一个唯一的哈希值，这使您可以将其用作其他字典或集合中的键。 然而，有时字典中包含的信息并不是一成不变的。 也就是说，它包含的值在创建后可以更改，因此无法生成稳定的哈希值。 发生这种情况时，Python 会引发“TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict'”错误消息。

此错误消息可能由多种因素触发，包括：

当您尝试将一个字典用作另一个字典的键时，Python 期望该字典是不可变的。 例如：

 dict1 = {} dict2 = {dict1: "value"}

在上面的示例中，Python 引发了 TypeError 因为 dict1 是可变的。

像字典一样，集合要求其元素是不可变的，以便可以对它们进行哈希处理。 当您将字典插入集合中，并且字典的内容在创建后可以更改时，Python 会引发 TypeError。 这是一个例子：

 set1 = set() set2 = {set1, {}}

如果您之前使用字典作为键，然后修改它，Python 将无法生成一致的哈希值，从而迫使它引发 TypeError。 这是一个例子：

 dict1 = {"key": "value"} dict2 = {dict1: "value1"} # Modify dict1 after it has been used as a key dict1["key"] = "value2" # Now try to use dict1 as a key again dict3 = {dict1: "value2"}

在上面的示例中，Python 在 dict3 上引发了 TypeError，因为 dict1 自首次用作键以来已被更改。

创建字典时，应确保值是不可变的。 如果这些值是可变的，而您稍后修改了它们，Python 将引发 TypeError。 这是一个例子：

 dict1 = {"key": []} dict2 = {dict1: "value"} # Now modify the mutable values in dict1 dict1["key"].append("value") dict3 = {dict1: "value2"}

在上面的示例中，Python 在 dict3 上引发了 TypeError，因为 dict1 中的值之一是可变的。

如何修复“TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict' in Python”

现在我们已经了解了“TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict' in Python”的一些常见原因，让我们看看如何解决它。

避免错误的一种方法是将字典解压缩到包含其键值对的元组中，从而使其不可变。 这是一个例子：

 dict1 = {} tuple1 = tuple(dict1.items()) dict2 = {tuple1: "value"}

在上面的示例中，我们将 dict1 转换为包含其键值对的元组，并将其用作 dict2 中的键。 这是可行的，因为元组是不可变的，并且包含可变元素的 dict1 的内容已被转换为不可变元组。

确保字典内容不可变的另一种方法是使用 frozenset。 这是一个例子：

 dict1 = {} frozenset1 = frozenset(dict1.items()) dict2 = {frozenset1: "value"}

在此示例中，我们已将 dict1 转换为 frozenset。 在我们的例子中，我们使用 dict.items() 方法将 dict1 转换为可迭代对象，然后将其传递给 frozenset。

如果上述方法对您不起作用，您可以创建一个自定义类来实现hash和eq方法。 这是一个例子：

 class CustomDict: def __init__(self, items): self.items = items def __hash__(self): return hash(frozenset(self.items.items())) def __eq__(self, other): return isinstance(other, CustomDict) and self.items == other.items dict1 = {} custom_dict = CustomDict(dict1) dict2 = {custom_dict: "value"}

在此示例中，我们创建了一个自定义字典类，它将字典作为输入并实现hash和eq方法。 最后的想法

但是，通过了解错误的常见原因，您可以采取必要的步骤来解决它。 在这篇博文中，我们探索了一些解决错误的方法，包括将字典解包为元组、使用 frozenset 以及创建自定义类。 通过实施这些解决方案，您可以避免 TypeError 并保持代码平稳运行。



原文:

Have you ever come across an error message that reads "TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict' in Python"? If yes, then you're not alone. This error occurs when you attempt to use a dictionary as the key of another dictionary or within a set. In this blog post, we will explore this error message in detail, understand what it means, and look at ways to fix it. So, let's get started.

The Error Message Explained

When you declare a dictionary in Python, it creates a unique hash value, which enables you to use it as a key in other dictionaries or sets. However, sometimes the information contained in a dictionary is not immutable. That is, the values it contains can be changed after it's created, making it impossible to generate a stable hash value. When this happens, Python raises a "TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict'" error message.

Common Causes of the "TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict' in Python"

This error message can be triggered by several factors, including:

1. Using a dictionary as a key of another dictionary

When you attempt to use a dictionary as the key of another dictionary, Python expects that the dictionary is immutable. For example:

dict1 = {}
dict2 = {dict1: "value"}

In the above example, Python raises a TypeError because dict1 is mutable.

2. Using a dictionary within a set

Like a dictionary, a set requires its elements to be immutable so that they can be hashed. When you insert a dictionary into a set, and the dictionary's contents can be changed after creation, Python raises a TypeError. Here's an example:

set1 = set()
set2 = {set1, {}}

Python raises TypeError because set1 is mutable.

3. Changing values in a dictionary used as a key

If you've used a dictionary as a key previously, and you then later modify it, Python won't be able to generate a consistent hash value, forcing it to raise a TypeError. Here's an example:

dict1 = {"key": "value"}
dict2 = {dict1: "value1"}
# Modify dict1 after it has been used as a key
dict1["key"] = "value2"
# Now try to use dict1 as a key again
dict3 = {dict1: "value2"}

In the above example, Python raises TypeError on dict3 because dict1 has been changed since it was first used as a key.

4. Creating a dictionary with mutable values

When you create a dictionary, you should ensure that the values are immutable. If the values are mutable, and you later modify them, Python will raise a TypeError. Here's an example:

dict1 = {"key": []}
dict2 = {dict1: "value"}

# Now modify the mutable values in dict1
dict1["key"].append("value")

dict3 = {dict1: "value2"}

In the example above, Python raises a TypeError on dict3 because one of the values in dict1 is mutable.

How to Fix the "TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict' in Python"

Now that we've seen some of the common causes of the "TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict' in Python," let's look at how we can resolve it.

1. Unpacking the dictionary into a tuple

One way to avoid the error is to unpack the dictionary into a tuple containing its key-value pairs, thus making it immutable. Here's an example:

dict1 = {}
tuple1 = tuple(dict1.items())
dict2 = {tuple1: "value"}

In the above example, we've converted dict1 into a tuple containing its key-value pairs and used it as a key in dict2. This works because tuples are immutable, and the contents of dict1, which contains mutable elements, have been converted into an immutable tuple.

2. Using frozenset

Another way to ensure that the contents of a dictionary are immutable is to use frozenset. Here's an example:

dict1 = {}
frozenset1 = frozenset(dict1.items())
dict2 = {frozenset1: "value"}

In this example, we've converted dict1 into a frozenset. The frozenset method takes an iterable and returns an immutable frozenset object. In our case, we've used the dict.items() method to convert dict1 into an iterable, which we've then passed to frozenset.

3. Creating a custom class

If the above methods don't work for you, you can create a custom class that implements the hash and eq methods. Here's an example:

class CustomDict:
    def __init__(self, items):
        self.items = items

    def __hash__(self):
        return hash(frozenset(self.items.items()))

    def __eq__(self, other):
        return isinstance(other, CustomDict) and self.items == other.items

dict1 = {}
custom_dict = CustomDict(dict1)
dict2 = {custom_dict: "value"}

In this example, we've created a custom dictionary class that takes a dictionary as input and implements the hash and eq methods. The hash method returns a hash value, which is generated by converting the dictionary's key-value pairs into a frozenset and hashing it. The eq method compares the CustomDict objects and returns True if they have the same key-value pairs.

Final Thoughts

The "TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict' in Python" can be frustrating, especially if you don't understand what it means or how to fix it. However, by understanding the common causes of the error, you can take the necessary steps to resolve it. In this blog post, we've explored some of the ways to resolve the error, including unpacking the dictionary into a tuple, using frozenset, and creating a custom class. By implementing these solutions, you can avoid the TypeError and keep your code running smoothly.

item.title: 单元测试和谷歌测试简介 | Introduction to Unit Testing and Google Testing
item.description:
苏玛7373

发表于 4 月 7 日

嘿，我是 Sushma BR，在 Luxoft 担任软件工程师。 在本文中，我试图介绍单元测试和 Google 测试框架。

测试：

测试是验证软件是否符合规范的过程。 开发人员将使用所有可能输入的选定子集来执行程序，并将实际输出与预期输出进行比较。

测试的好处包括

  • 防止错误

  • 降低开发成本和提高性能等..

什么是单元测试？

单元测试是一种软件测试，其中测试软件的各个单元或组件。 目的是验证软件代码的每个单元是否按预期执行。 单元测试由开发人员在应用程序的开发（编码阶段）期间完成。 单元测试隔离一段代码并验证其正确性。 一个单元可以是一个单独的函数、方法、过程、模块或对象。
在 SDLC、STLC、V 模型中，单元测试是在集成测试之前完成的第一级测试。 单元测试是一种白盒测试技术，通常由开发人员执行。

为什么要进行单元测试？

  • 单元测试很重要，因为软件开发人员有时会尝试通过最少的单元测试来节省时间，而这是神话，因为不适当的单元测试会导致在系统测试、集成测试甚至应用程序构建后的 Beta 测试期间修复缺陷的成本很高。 如果在早期开发中进行了适当的单元测试，那么最终会节省时间和金钱。

  • 显然，测试可以提高代码的质量，它可以帮助开发人员理解测试代码库并使他们能够快速进行更改。

  • 单元测试有助于代码重用。

  • 在添加行为或进行修复时让开发人员充满信心。

如何进行单元测试？

为了进行单元测试，开发人员编写一段代码来测试软件应用程序中的特定功能。 开发人员还可以隔离此功能以进行更严格的测试，从而揭示被测功能与其他单元之间不必要的依赖关系，从而消除依赖关系。 开发人员一般使用单元测试框架来开发自动化测试用例进行单元测试。
单元测试简单地说，它们是支持编写和运行单元测试的软件工具，包括构建测试的基础以及执行测试和报告结果的功能。

什么是谷歌测试？

  • 它是一个测试框架，google test 是一个用于编写 C++ 单元测试的库，或者 Google test 是一种用于编写和运行单元测试的软件工具。

  • 在谷歌测试中，代码是逐行测试的。

  • 它基于 xUnit 架构，这是一组用于编程和自动执行测试用例的框架。

为什么谷歌测试？

  • 独立且可重复：谷歌测试通过在不同的对象上运行每个测试来隔离测试。

  • 可移植和可重用：Google 测试适用于不同的操作系统（Linux、Windows 或 Mac），具有不同的编译器。

  • 当测试失败时，它会提供有关问题的大量信息，因此很容易解决问题。

断言是检查条件是否为真的语句。 断言的结果可以是

注意：- 如果发生致命故障，它将中止当前函数，否则程序将继续正常运行。

测试中的断言用于验证被测试代码的行为。 如果测试有一个失败的断言，那么它将失败，否则它会成功。

这些断言进行基本的真/假条件测试。

谷歌测试的理性运算符列表：

简单测试

编写测试用例：

  • 测试文件应该有#include "gtest/gtest.h"

  • 使用宏 TEST_F() 来定义和命名测试函数，这些是不返回值的普通 C++ 函数。

  • 我们可以使用各种 Google 测试断言来检查测试值。

  • 测试结果将基于断言，如果测试中的任何断言失败则整个测试失败，否则它成功。

注意：- 可以在单个测试用例中编写多个断言。

例子：-

类 SumTest: public ::testing::Test { public: SumTest() {}; 虚拟 ~SumTest() {}; 虚拟无效设置（）{}; 虚拟无效拆解（）{};

返回 RUN_ALL_TESTS()；

谷歌测试环境：

实际测试用例：

结论：

在文章中，我解释了单元测试和谷歌测试的基础知识。 本文只是粗浅地介绍了 Google C++ 测试框架。 有关该框架的详细文档可从 Google 站点获得。

如果 sushma7373 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

原文:

Sushma7373

Posted on Apr 7

Hey, I am Sushma B R, working as software engineer at Luxoft. In this article, I have tried to give a Introduction to Unit Testing and Google Testing Framework.

Testing:

Testing is the process of verifying the software performs to the specifications. The developer will execute the program with selected subset of all the possible input, and compares the actual output with the expected output.

Benefits of testing include

  • Preventing bugs

  • Reducing development costs and improving performance so on..

What is Unit Testing?

Unit testing is a type of software testing where individual units or components of a software are tested. The purpose is to validate that each unit of the software code performs as expected. Unit Testing is done during the development (coding phase) of an application by the developers. Unit Tests isolate a section of code and verify its correctness. A unit may be an individual function, method, procedure, module, or object.
In SDLC, STLC, V Model, Unit testing is first level of testing done before integration testing. Unit testing is a White Box testing technique that is usually performed by the developer.

Why unit testing?

  • Unit Testing is important because software developers sometimes try saving time doing minimal unit testing and this is myth because inappropriate unit testing leads to high cost Defect fixing during System Testing, Integration Testing and even Beta Testing after application is built. If proper unit testing is done in early development, then it saves time and money in the end.

  • Obviously tests improve the quality of your code, it helps the developers to understand the testing code base and enables them to make changes quickly.

  • Unit tests help with code re-use.

  • Give developers confidence when adding behavior or making fixes.

How to do Unit Testing?

In order to do Unit Testing, developers write a section of code to test a specific function in software application. Developers can also isolate this function to test more rigorously which reveals unnecessary dependencies between function being tested and other units so the dependencies can be eliminated. Developers generally use Unit Test framework to develop automated test cases for unit testing.
The Unit Testing simply stated, they are software tools to support writing and running unit tests, including a foundation on which to build tests and the functionality to execute the tests and report their results.

What is Google testing?

  • It is a test framework, The google test is a library for writing the unit test for C++ or Google test is a one type of software tool for writing as well as running the unit test.

  • In google testing the code is tested line-by-line.

  • It is based on xUnit architecture which is a set of Frameworks for programming and automated execution of test cases.

Why Google testing?

  • Google Test helps us to write better C++ tests.

  • Independent and Repeatable: Google test isolates the tests by running each of them on a different object.

  • Portable and Reusable: Google test works on different Oses (Linux, Windows, or a Mac), with different compilers.

  • When tests fail, it will provide much information about the problem, so it will be easy to solve the issues.

Basic concepts and Assertions in Google test :
Assertions are statements that check whether a condition is true. An assertion's result can be

2.Nonfatal failure, or fatal failure

Note :- If the fatal failure occurs, it will abort the current function else the program continues normally.

Assertions in tests used to verify the behavior of the tested code. If a test has a failed assertion, then it fails else it succeeds.

These assertions do basic true/false condition testing.

List of rational operator for Google testing :

Simple tests

To write test cases:

  • The test file shoulde be having the #include "gtest/gtest.h"

  • Use the macro TEST_F() for defining and naming a test function, These are ordinary C++ functions that don't return a value.

  • We can use various Google Test assertions to check values of the tests.

  • Test results will be based on assertions, if any assertions in the test fails Then the entire test fails else it succeeds.

Note :- Can write many assertions in single test case.

Example:-

class SumTest: public ::testing::Test { public: SumTest() {}; virtual ~SumTest() {}; /// SetUp will be called before each test that uses this fixture. virtual void SetUp() {}; /// Will be called after each test if the test uses this fixture. virtual void TearDown() {};

};

// Test of sum. TEST_F(SumTest, Test_T1) { EXPECT_EQ(2, sum(1 + 1)); EXPECT_LT(3, sum(2 + 2)); EXPECT_GT(4, sum(3 + 0); EXPECT_NQ(5, sum(8 + 1);

}

int main(int argc, char **argv) { ::testing::InitGoogleTest(&argc, argv); return RUN_ALL_TESTS();

}

Google test environment:

Actual testcases:

Conclusion:

In the article i explained basics of the unit testing and google testing. Getting started with Google Test is pretty easy.It has more features than other tests.Google test can be used to prevent the bugs at the intial stage, which will reduce the more debuging time and cost in the end.
This article just scratches the surface of the Google C++ Testing Framework. Detailed documentation about the framework is available from the Google site.

Arindam Chowdhury - Apr 6

解除暂停后，jacksonkasi 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，jacksonkasi 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

item.title: การสร้าง聊天机器人电报ที่ใช้gpt 3.5ในpython | การสร้าง chat bot Telegram ที่ใช้ gpt 3.5 ใน python
item.description:
请求 OpenAI API

  • 大家好！

    请发送sendmessage ให้เป็นดังนี้:

     def sendmessage(chatid, message): headers = {"Content-Type": "application/json", "Authorization": f"Bearer {OPEN_AI}"} info = { # The model used (you can use gpt-4 if you have access to it) "model": "gpt-3.5-turbo", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": message}], # Randomness of the result "temperature": 0.7, "max_tokens": 1024 } post_request = requests.post("https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions", headers=headers, json=info) response_api = post_request.json()["choices"][0]["message"]["content"] url = f"https://api.telegram.org/bot{BOT_TOKEN}/sendMessage" payload = { "text": response_api, "chat_id": chatid }

    详细信息

    • 下载 Space Cli curl -fsSL https://get.deta.dev/space-cli.sh | sh curl -fsSL https://get.deta.dev/space-cli.sh | sh
    • 一旦暂停，ffrsr 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    • 解除暂停后，ffrsr 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，ffrsr 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 ffrsr 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 ffrsr：

    降低 ffrsr 的所有帖子的可见度

    报告其他不当行为

    取消标记 ffrsr 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    ขั้นตอนการสร้าง Telegram chat bot ที่ทำงานร่วมกับ OpenAI API

    1. ตั้งค่า Flask บนเครื่องคอมพิวเตอร์ของคุณ
    2. สร้าง Telegram Bot
    3. การเชื่อมต่อ Flask กับ Telegram Bot
    4. การเขียนฟังก์ชันเพื่อ request ไปยัง OpenAI API
    5. การใช้บน Deta.space

    เมื่อทำตามขั้นตอนเหล่านี้ จะทำให้คุณสามารถสร้าง Telegram Bot ที่สามารถโต้ตอบกับผู้ใช้ได้อย่างชาญฉลาด โดยการใช้ประโยชน์จาก OpenAI มาเริ่มกันเลย!!

    1. ตั้งค่า Flask บนเครื่องคอมพิวเตอร์ของคุณ 

    mkdir telegrambot
cd telegrambot
touch main.py

# Open the file in the editor that u prefer

    จากนั้นให้ติดตั้งโมดูลที่จำเป็นลงไป ทีนี้คุณจะต้องเลือกว่าจะใช้ virtual env. หรือ local machine สมมติคุณจะใช้ virtual env. ก็ให้ set up ด้วยคำสั่งดังต่อไปนี้:

    python -m venv <name_of_virtual_environment>

# if you use bash
source <name_of_virtual_environment>

# if you use fish
source <name_of_virtual_environment>/bin/activate.fish

    ทีนี้เปิดไฟล์ main.py และเขียนโค้ดพื้นฐานกัน main.py

    from flask import Flask, request

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route("/", methods=["GET"])
def index():
    return "Hello World!"


if __name__ == "__main__":
    port = 5000
    app.run(debug=True, host='0.0.0.0', port=port)

    ต่อไปให้ติดตั้ง flask ผ่าน pip: pip3 install flask
    หลังจากติดตั้งแล้ว เราสามารถรันแอปได้ด้วยคำสั่ง: python3 main.py
    จะเห็นว่าเราสร้างแอปให้ทำงานได้แล้ว!

    2. สร้าง Telegram Bot

    ในการเชื่อมต่อ Flask ของเรากับ Telegram Bot API ก่อนอื่นเราต้องสร้าง Bot ใหม่บน Telegram ซึ่งเราสามารถทำได้ง่าย ๆ โดยทำตามขั้นตอนดังนี้

    1. เปิด Telegram และค้นหา " @botfather " ในแถบค้นหา
    2. เริ่มการสนทนากับ BotFather โดยส่งคำสั่ง "/ start"
    3. หากต้องการสร้าง Bot ใหม่ ให้พิมพ์คำสั่ง "/newbot" และทำตามคำแนะนำของ BotFather
    4. ตั้งชื่อ Bot ของคุณและชื่อผู้ใช้ที่ลงท้ายด้วย "bot"
    5. เมื่อคุณสร้าง Bot สำเร็จแล้ว BotFather จะให้ token การอนุญาตแก่คุณ ซึ่งคุณจะต้องใช้เพื่อสื่อสารกับ Telegram Bot API

    ด้วยการสร้าง Bot ใหม่บน Telegram เราสามารถเชื่อมโยงมันกับ Flask ของเราและเริ่มสร้างฟังก์ชันการทำงานของ chat Bot ได้

    3. หลังจากสร้าง Bot และได้รับ Bot token แล้ว ตอนนี้เราสามารถเชื่อมโยงมันกับ Flask ของเราได้แล้ว

    เราสามารถใช้โมดูลrequests ได้โดยติดตั้งแพ็คเกจโดยใช้คำสั่ง pip3 install requests

    ทีนี้มาดูโค้ด main.py ที่อัปเดตแล้วกัน:

    import requests
from flask import Flask, request

app = Flask(__name__)

BOT_TOKEN = <your_token_value>

def sendmessage(chatid, message):
    url = f"https://api.telegram.org/bot{BOT_TOKEN}/sendMessage"
    payload = {
        "text": message,
        "chat_id": chatid
    }

    print(requests.get(url, params=payload))

@app.route("/", methods=["POST", "GET"])
def index():
    if (request.method == "POST"):
        resp = request.get_json()
        print(resp)
        # We get the content of the message
        msgtext = resp["message"]["text"]
        # We get the id of the chat
        chatid = resp["message"]["chat"]["id"]
        # The message is sent back
        sendmessage(chatid, msgtext)
        return "Done"
    else:
        return "Hello World!"

if __name__ == "__main__":
    port = 5000
    app.run(debug=True, host='0.0.0.0', port=port)

    ก่อนที่จะทดลองว่า Bot ทำงานหรือไม่ เราต้องใช้ngrokเพื่อตั้งค่า HTTP Tunnel เป็น Localhost โดยใช้คำสั่ง ngrok http 5000


    เราสามารถตรวจสอบได้ว่า URL ใช้การได้หรือไม่โดยใช้คำสั่งcurl

    # telegrambot> curl https://a577-131-114-63-2.eu.ngrok.io
Hello World!⏎

    ตอนนี้เราสามารถตั้งค่า webHook ของ Telegram Bot ได้แล้ว:

    curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/setwebHook?url=https://a577-131-114-63-2.eu.ngrok.io"
{"ok":true,"result":true,"description":"Webhook was set"}⏎

    และเราจะเห็นว่าตอนนี้ Bot ทำงานได้อย่างสมบูรณ์

    4. การเขียนฟังก์ชันที่เรียก openAI API

    ในการรับรหัส API เราต้องสร้างบัญชีบน OpenAI หลังจากนั้นเราไปที่ URL นี้ platform.openai.com/account/api-keys
    เพื่อรับรหัสของเราเอง

    ทีนี้เราก็อัปเดตฟังก์ชัน sendmessage ให้เป็นดังนี้:

    def sendmessage(chatid, message):
    headers = {"Content-Type": "application/json",
               "Authorization": f"Bearer {OPEN_AI}"}
    info = {
        # The model used (you can use gpt-4 if you have access to it)
        "model": "gpt-3.5-turbo",
        "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": message}],
        # Randomness of the result
        "temperature": 0.7,
        "max_tokens": 1024
    }
    post_request = requests.post("https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions", headers=headers, json=info)

    response_api = post_request.json()["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]
    url = f"https://api.telegram.org/bot{BOT_TOKEN}/sendMessage"
    payload = {
        "text": response_api,
        "chat_id": chatid
    }

    ปรับใช้บน deta.space

    ในการปรับใช้บน deta.space เราจำเป็นต้องทำตามขั้นตอนเหล่านี้

    • สร้างบัญชี
    • ดาวน์โหลด Space Cli curl -fsSL https://get.deta.dev/space-cli.sh | sh
    • เข้าสู่ระบบบน cli ด้วย space login
    • สร้างโครงการด้วย space new
    • อัปเดตโค้ดสองสามบรรทัดให้เป็นดังนี้ 
     import os
 OPEN_AI = os.getenv("OPEN_AI")
 BOT_TOKEN = os.getenv("BOT_TOKEN")
    • การสร้าง Spacefile วิธีเรียกใช้แอปดังนี้ 
    # Spacefile Docs: https://go.deta.dev/docs/spacefile/v0
v: 0
app_name: telegrambot
micros:
    - name: telegrambot
      src: .
      engine: python3.9
      primary: true
      public_routes:
        - "/"
      presets:
        env:
          - name: BOT_TOKEN
            description: Telegram Bot Key
            default: "<BOT_TOKEN>"
          - name: OPEN_AI
            description: Open AI Key
            default: "<OPEN_AI_API>"
    • กดแอปไปที่ deta.space ด้วยspace push
    • การเปลี่ยน hook ของ telegram ด้วยคำสั่งเดียวกันแต่ใช้ url ของ deta.space แทน

    เสร็จเรียบร้อย
    เราได้สร้าง Bot ที่สวยงามซึ่งใช้ปัญญาประดิษฐ์ในการช่วยเหลือคุณ

    Kenichiro Nakamura - Mar 5

    Once suspended, ffrsr will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, ffrsr will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by ffrsr will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If ffrsr is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Note:

    Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag ffrsr:

    Make all posts by ffrsr less visible

    ffrsr consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging ffrsr will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 使用物联网自定义您的业务流程 | Customize Your Business Process with IoT
item.description:
随着物联网 (IoT) 的不断发展，我们在日常生活中与数字设备的互动越来越多。

使用“智能”设备，用户可以通过将普通设备转变为“智能”设备并进行无线通信来修改甚至自动化这些普通设备的功能。 由此产生的个人和企业都有无限的可能性。

为何如此？ 在许多现实世界的例子中，智能技术使企业受益匪浅。 使用物联网应用程序，零售店正在收集有价值的消费者数据，分析他们的购物行为和购买模式，并相应地调整他们的业务——甚至改变商店中流行产品的摆放位置，以改善客户体验并增加销售额。

开发物联网应用程序和定制软件可以提高企业的安全性、安全性、生产力和效率。 以下是一些利用物联网改善业务的方法。

物联网对企业的好处：

工作场所的安全
智能相机和运动传感器可以保护您业务的各个领域。 该软件随这些设备一起提供，可以安装在台式机和移动设备上。

如果有任何异常或不正常的行为，相应的设备将通过电子邮件、短信或即时消息通知您，以便您在问题升级之前做出响应。 担心盗窃、防损和未经授权的违规行为的企业主将从这项服务中受益。

简化库存管理
使用物联网，制造业正在提高他们创造的产品的质量，并更有效地管理他们的库存。 通过在整个制造过程中监控产品质量，他们可以检测任何异常情况，并保持设备和程序的标准。 另一个好处是，他们会提前收到可能的库存短缺警报，并可以下订单以确保他们需要的东西可用。

降低成本
行业特定的物联网解决方案简化了制造、供应链、生产和其他行业的流程。 通过简化操作，可最大限度地减少停机时间并降低成本。

制造业是物联网技术如何降低成本的一个很好的例子。

提高生产力和盈利能力
通过自动化工作流程，物联网有助于优化工作流程。 该系统通过最大限度地减少重复性任务来促进机器和资源的最佳利用。

组织可以通过优化运营来减少工时浪费。

据物联网支持的组织称，员工的工作效率更高，支出减少了 83%。

安全的工作场所
在全球范围内，本可预防的工作场所事故每天都在发生。 可以通过使用智能技术来防止灾难发生。 通过使用智能设备，工作人员可以减少人为错误，检测工业设备的异常情况，并提前提醒潜在的危险。 如果工作场所或设备出现问题，正确的物联网开发应用程序可以让用户立即收到通知，然后采取必要的行动——是关闭设备、疏散，还是暂时限制员工行动，直到该区域安全为止- 解决问题。

物联网对您的企业来说是一个不错的选择吗？
物联网已成为近年来最有前途的数字技术之一，未来很可能被多个行业采用。 尽管存在风险，但如果您研究您的选择并仔细规划您的预算，您可以从实施物联网中获益匪浅。

借助智能技术，您可以自动化您的库存管理实践、监控员工士气和满意度，并使用智能传感器让您的工作空间更加高效和舒适。 只需确定您想要解决的问题、您希望实现的结果以及如何实现这些目标。

行业特定的物联网解决方案
让我们来看看一些为特定行业量身定制的物联网解决方案。 在软件设计师的帮助下开发高度特定的物联网系统可以提高效率、生产力和安全性，同时最大限度地减少停机时间。 任何行业都可以从定制解决方案中受益，无论您的公司是在医疗保健、农业、制造、零售还是工业领域。

库存管理、减少废物和资产跟踪

您是否在管理库存或跟踪资产方面遇到困难？ 您是否必须花费大量资金来更换丢失的资产？ 员工是否有可能在与库存相关的问题上花费过多的时间和资源？ 随着资产和库存在整个供应链中移动，定制的物联网应用程序可以自动跟踪和控制它们。 监控端到端操作使您能够识别并加强任何弱点。 通过在您的仓库和存储单元中使用物联网，您可以自动跟踪库存变化。 除了防止盗窃外，这些系统还可以帮助定位和找回丢失的资产。

简化的客户体验和实时业务洞察

当您将自定义物联网系统集成到业务运营的多个方面时，就会创建数据流。 数据访问带来更好的分析潜力，从而使公司更深入地了解如何改善其运营。 您还需要充分了解消费者如何使用您的产品和服务。 通过充分了解消费者如何使用他们的产品，公司可以更好地满足他们的需求，同时改善客户服务。

零售企业可以使用定制的物联网解决方案来减少购买过程中的摩擦。 很多时候，这种情况发生在购买物品之前的增强现实或虚拟现实环境中，例如想象一套衣服的外观或一件家具如何适合他们的家。

其他公司，包括亚马逊和总部位于英国的杂货商塞恩斯伯里 (Sainsbury's)，已采用“购物、扫描和走”的方法来消除排队。 使用智能手机应用程序，您可以扫描购买的商品并在完成后离开商店。 当您离开场所时，该应用程序可以相应地向您收费。 这不仅消除了排队等候，还让企业更好地了解顾客如何购物和在店内走动。

如何启动您的物联网项目？
在规划物联网项目时，最好找一家专门从事物联网软件开发的公司。 集成硬件和自定义物联网应用程序是其端到端物联网开发服务的一部分。 根据您的技术和业务要求，他们可以开发适合您的需求和预算的定制解决方案。 为确保您的系统提供最佳的用户体验，他们提供发布后支持和更新。

结论
也许物联网最大的优势在于它的灵活性和适应任何行业的能力。 定制的物联网系统和一点创造力将为您的业务带来可衡量的收益。 无论您的预算如何，您都可以扩展 IoT 解决方案以适合您公司的规模。 一些研究和数据点表明物联网领域的扩张速度有多快，以及它对公司发展的积极影响。

来源网址： hashstudioztechnology.blogspot.com/2023 ...



原文:


As the Internet of Things (IoT) continues to develop, we are increasingly interacting with digital devices in everyday life.

Using 'smart' devices, users can modify and even automate the functionality of otherwise ordinary devices by transforming them into 'smart' devices and communicating wirelessly. There are endless possibilities for both individuals and businesses resulting from this.

How so? Smart technology has significantly benefited businesses in many real-world examples. Using IoT apps, retail stores are collecting valuable consumer data, analyzing their shopping behavior and purchase patterns, and adjusting their businesses accordingly - even changing the place where popular products are placed in stores in order to improve customer experience and increase sales.

Developing IoT applications and custom software can improve safety, security, productivity, and efficiency for businesses. Here are a few ways to improve your business with the Internet of Things.

Benefits of IoT for businesses:

Security at workplace
Various areas of your business can be protected with smart cameras and motion sensors. The software comes with these devices, which can be installed on desktops and mobile devices.

The appropriate device will notify you via email, text message, or instant message if there is any abnormal or irregular behavior, so you can respond before it escalates. Owners of businesses who are concerned about theft, loss prevention, and unauthorized breaches will benefit from this service.

Streamlined inventory management
Using IoT, the manufacturing sector is improving the quality of the products they create and managing their inventories more efficiently. By monitoring the quality of their products throughout the manufacturing process, they can detect any anomalies, and maintain the standard of their equipment and procedures. A further benefit is that they are alerted beforehand of possible stock shortages and can place an order to ensure that what they need is available.

Cost reduction
Industry-specific IoT solutions streamline processes in manufacturing, supply chain, production, and other industries. By streamlining operations, downtime is minimized and costs are reduced.

Manufacturing is a good example of how IoT technology can reduce costs.

Boost productivity and profitability
By automating workflows, IoT helps optimize workflows. The system facilitates optimal utilization of machines and resources by minimizing repetitive tasks.

It is possible for organizations to reduce man-hour waste by optimizing their operations.

According to IoT-powered organizations, employees are more productive and expenditures are reduced by 83%.

Safe workplaces
Globally, preventable workplace accidents occur every day. It is possible to prevent disasters from occurring by using smart technology. By using smart devices, staff can reduce human error, detect abnormalities in industrial equipment, and be alerted to potential hazards ahead of time. In the event of an issue with the workplace or equipment, the right IoT development application allows users to be notified immediately, and then take the necessary actions - whether to shut down the equipment, evacuate, or temporarily restrict staff movement until the area is safe - to resolve the issue.

Is IoT a good choice for your business?
IoT has become one of the most promising digital technologies in recent years, and it is likely to be adopted across multiple industries in the future. Even though there are risks involved, you can greatly benefit from implementing IoT if you research your options and carefully plan your budget.

With smart technology, you can automate your inventory management practices, monitor employee morale and satisfaction, and use smart sensors to make your workspace more efficient and comfortable. Simply identify the problems you want to solve, the outcomes you hope to achieve, and how to get there.

Industry-specific IoT solutions
Let's take a look at some of the IoT solutions tailored to specific industries. Developing highly specific IoT systems with the help of software designers can boost efficiency, productivity, and security while minimizing downtime. Any industry can benefit from custom solutions, whether your company is in the healthcare, agriculture, manufacturing, retail, or industrial spheres.

Inventory management, waste reduction, and asset tracking

Are you having trouble managing your inventory or tracking your assets? Do you have to spend a lot of money replacing lost assets? Is it possible that employees are spending excessive time and resources on inventory-related issues? As assets and inventory move throughout your supply chain, custom IoT applications can track and control them automatically. Monitoring end-to-end operations allow you to identify and strengthen any weaknesses. By using IoT in your warehouses and storage units, you can keep track of inventory shifts automatically. These systems can also help locate and recover lost assets in addition to preventing theft.

Streamlined customer experience & real-time business insights

Data streams are created when you integrate custom IoT systems into multiple aspects of your business operations. Data access leads to better analytics potential, which gives companies greater insights into how to improve their operations. You need to be well informed about how your products and services are used by consumers as well. By fully understanding how consumers use their products, companies can better meet their needs while improving customer service.

Retail businesses can use custom IoT solutions to reduce friction in the buying process. A lot of times, this happens in an augmented or virtual reality environment before purchasing the item, such as visualizing how an outfit would look or how a piece of furniture would fit in their home.

Other companies, including Amazon and UK-based grocer Sainsbury's, have adopted the 'shop, scan, and go' method to eliminate lines. With a smartphone app, you can scan your purchases and leave the store whenever you're done. When you leave the premises, the app can bill you accordingly. This not only eliminates waiting in lines but also gives the business a better understanding of how customers shop and move around the store.

How to start your IoT project?
When planning an IoT project, it's best to approach a company that specializes in Internet of Things software development. HashStudioz Technologies Inc. is an IoT app development company based in the USA. Integrated hardware and custom IoT applications are part of their end-to-end IoT development services. Depending on your technical and business requirements, they can develop a customized solution that suits both your needs and your budget. To ensure that your system delivers the best possible user experience, they provide post-launch support and updates.

Conclusion
Perhaps IoT's greatest strength is its flexibility and ability to adapt to any industry. A custom IoT system and a bit of creativity will yield measurable benefits for your business. You can scale IoT solutions to suit your company's size regardless of your budget. Several studies and data points indicate how fast the IoT sphere is expanding, as well as the positive impacts it can have on a company's growth.

item.title: Node.js 在物联网 (IoT) 开发中的未来 | The Future of Node.js in Internet of Things (IoT) Development
item.description:
福拉萨约塞缪尔

发表于 4 月 7 日

物联网 (IoT) 行业已经认识到 Node.js 的潜力，并越来越多地将其用于 IoT 开发。 在本文中，我们将讨论 Node.js 在 IoT 开发中的未来以及它为何会改变游戏规则。

从智能家居设备到工业传感器，物联网设备正在改变我们与技术互动的方式。 然而，构建物联网解决方案并非易事。 开发人员在构建可扩展、安全且可靠的 IoT 应用程序时面临多项挑战。 以下是 Node.js 是物联网开发的未来的一些原因。

  • 可扩展性：Node.js 建立在 V8 JavaScript 引擎之上，该引擎以其性能和可扩展性着称。 这使得 Node.js 成为构建需要处理大量数据的物联网应用程序的理想平台。

  • 轻量级：物联网设备的资源有限，例如内存和处理能力。 这使其成为构建需要低内存和 CPU 使用率的 IoT 应用程序的绝佳选择。

  • 易于学习：Node.js 基于 JavaScript，它是世界上最流行的编程语言之一。 熟悉 JavaScript 的开发人员可以快速学习 Node.js 并开始构建物联网应用程序。

  • 社区支持：Node.js 有一个庞大而活跃的社区，为构建 IoT 应用程序提供支持、更新和库。 这使开发人员可以轻松找到问题的解决方案并快速构建应用程序。

  • 安全性：安全性是物联网开发中的一个关键问题。 物联网发展的未来在于能够快速构建可扩展、安全和可靠的应用程序。 凭借其可扩展性、轻量级特性、易于学习、社区支持和安全功能，Node.js 有望成为物联网开发的首选平台。

    总之，Node.js 是物联网开发的未来。 它的可扩展性、轻量级特性、易于学习、社区支持和安全功能使其成为构建物联网应用程序的理想平台。 随着物联网行业的持续发展，Node.js 将在塑造物联网发展的未来方面发挥重要作用。

    谢谢阅读...
    快乐编码！

原文:

FOLASAYO SAMUEL

Posted on Apr 7

Node.js has been one of the most popular platforms for server-side programming since its inception in 2009. However, its applications are no longer limited to server-side development. The Internet of Things (IoT) industry has recognized the potential of Node.js and is increasingly adopting it for IoT development. In this article, we will discuss the future of Node.js in IoT development and why it is a game-changer.

IoT devices are becoming more common, and their popularity is growing day by day. From smart home devices to industrial sensors, IoT devices are changing the way we interact with technology. However, building IoT solutions is not an easy task. Developers face several challenges in building scalable, secure, and reliable IoT applications. Node.js, with its asynchronous, event-driven architecture, is a perfect fit for building IoT applications. Here are some reasons why Node.js is the future of IoT development.

  • Scalability: Node.js is built on the V8 JavaScript engine, which is known for its performance and scalability. Node.js provides a non-blocking I/O model, which allows it to handle a large number of connections concurrently. This makes Node.js an ideal platform for building IoT applications that need to handle large amounts of data.

  • Lightweight: IoT devices have limited resources, such as memory and processing power. Node.js is lightweight and can run on devices with limited resources. This makes it an excellent choice for building IoT applications that require low memory and CPU usage.

  • Easy to learn: Node.js is based on JavaScript, which is one of the most popular programming languages in the world. Developers who are familiar with JavaScript can quickly learn Node.js and start building IoT applications.

  • Community support: Node.js has a large and active community that provides support, updates, and libraries for building IoT applications. This makes it easy for developers to find solutions to their problems and build applications quickly.

  • Security: Security is a critical concern in IoT development. Node.js has built-in security features, such as the ability to sandbox applications, which makes it a secure platform for building IoT applications.

The future of IoT development lies in the ability to build scalable, secure, and reliable applications quickly. Node.js is an ideal platform for achieving this goal. With its scalability, lightweight nature, ease of learning, community support, and security features, Node.js is poised to become the platform of choice for IoT development.

In conclusion, Node.js is the future of IoT development. Its scalability, lightweight nature, ease of learning, community support, and security features make it an ideal platform for building IoT applications. As the IoT industry continues to grow, Node.js will play a significant role in shaping the future of IoT development.

Thanks for reading...
Happy Coding!

chandra penugonda - Mar 20

item.title: 危险品运输培训课程和认证 | Transportation of Dangerous Goods Training Course and Certification
item.description:
随着加拿大各地危险品运输的增加，优先考虑工人和公众的安全至关重要。 在运输过程中对危险货物处理不当可能导致毁灭性事故，对公共安全构成重大威胁。 这就是为什么参与危险材料运输的公司、管理人员和工人接受必要的培训以确保正确处理和运输至关重要。

可用于此目的的一项基本培训计划是危险品运输 (TDG) 培训课程。 这一综合性在线课程为学习者提供了他们需要了解的有关一般 TDG 培训要求的所有指南。 通过本课程，学习者可以获得所需的培训，从而有资格和自信地处理危险材料。

灵活性是 TDG 培训课程的关键，允许学习者随时暂停他们的进度以适应他们的日程安排。 此功能对于那些工作日程繁忙且有其他承诺的人特别有用。 此外，该课程有完整的解说，并且符合安大略省残疾人法案 (AODA) 的无障碍标准。

投资于 TDG 培训对于参与危险材料运输的个人和公司都至关重要。 通过学习本课程，学习者可以获得必要的知识和技能，以确保危险货物的安全处理和运输。 此外，TDG 培训课程可以帮助公司满足监管要求并避免代价高昂的处罚，从而使公司受益。 适当的培训和合规性可以减少事故和事件，这有助于保护工人和公众、提高公司声誉并节省时间和金钱。

综上所述，危险品的运输是许多行业的一个重要方面，在处理危险品时必须将安全放在首位。 通过投资于 TDG 培训，个人和公司可以提高安全性、合规性，并最终保护公众及其员工。



原文:


The Importance of Transportation of Dangerous Goods Training: A Comprehensive Guide

With the transportation of hazardous materials across Canada on the rise, it's crucial to prioritize the safety of workers and the public. Mishandling dangerous goods during transportation can lead to devastating accidents that pose significant threats to public safety. That's why it's crucial for companies, management, and workers involved in the transportation of hazardous materials to receive the necessary training to ensure proper handling and transport.

One essential training program available for this purpose is the Transportation of Dangerous Goods (TDG) training course. This comprehensive online course provides learners with all the guidelines they need to know about general TDG training requirements. With this course, learners can receive the training they need to become qualified and confident in handling hazardous materials.

Flexibility is key with the TDG training course, allowing learners to pause their progress at any time to suit their schedule. This feature is especially helpful for those with busy work schedules and other commitments. Additionally, the course is fully narrated, and it meets the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA) standards.

Investing in TDG training is essential for both individuals and companies involved in the transportation of hazardous materials. By taking this course, learners gain the necessary knowledge and skills to ensure the safe handling and transportation of dangerous goods. The TDG course covers everything from the classification and identification of dangerous goods to packaging and labeling requirements, documentation, emergency response plans, and more.

Furthermore, the TDG training course can benefit companies by helping them meet regulatory requirements and avoid costly penalties. Proper training and compliance can lead to reduced accidents and incidents, which can help protect workers and the public, improve company reputation, and save time and money.

In summary, the transportation of dangerous goods is a critical aspect of many industries, and it's essential to prioritize safety when handling hazardous materials. The TDG training course provides comprehensive guidelines and knowledge to ensure safe transportation and handling of dangerous goods. By investing in TDG training, individuals and companies can improve safety, compliance, and ultimately protect the public and their workers.

item.title: Brainy pi 的故事 - 无所不知 | The story of Brainy pi - Know it all
item.description:
此博客将告诉您为什么存在 brainy pi？ 未解决的问题，创新的方法，为什么选择我们，与同行的比较以及 Brainy pi 的未来。 你会在它的最后知道这一切。

为什么 Brainy pi 存在？
未解决的问题：很多商业想法可以用定制硬件做得更好。与软件相比，硬件设计仍然很困难。通过集成不同模块构建定制软件已经变得普遍和容易，这带来了如此多的利基软件解决方案。我们能得到这个吗软件到硬件设计的模块化是否有助于将更多下一代人工智能和物联网产品更快地推向市场？ 但从原型到量产仍然是一段艰难的设计之旅，许多 kickstarter 产品从未交付。 人类失去了如此多的创意。

图片来源： qualityinspection.org

解决方案：我们能否获得 2 个世界中最好的？ 原型制作时的速度，可扩展的制造设计？
新方法： Brainy Pi 是一个完整的硬件和软件解决方案，弥合了原型设计和大规模生产之间的差距。 它利用 Raspberry pi 的软件生态系统进行快速原型制作，但可针对大规模生产进行定制。

代码怎么样？ 如果硬件被定制或更改，我们是否必须重新编写所有代码？

神奇之处：由 Shunya os 团队支持的 Brainy pi 可以确保为 Brainy pi / Raspberry pi 编写的所有代码都可以顺利移植到任何基于 Arm(Cortex-A) 的硬件中，在配置和配置方面提供广泛的 SoC（芯片）选择他们最终产品的定价。

还是 Brainy Pi 只是 Raspberry Pi 的另一个克隆？有什么不同？ 一句话概括：稳定、可靠、长期可用、有据可查和支持，专注于严谨的产品开发人员和企业家。 目的不是推出和销售过多的板，而是制作稳定可靠的板并长期维护它，作为在其上构建出色产品的首选平台。

你能再比较一下吗？
核心芯片RK3399被许多来自谷歌Chromebook、华硕Tinkerboard 2、许多中国厂商Orange、Nano、Rock等pi的使用。 他们都是以瑞芯微的平板电脑参考设计为基础，然后根据自己的目标进行修改。 因此，在硬件方面，Brainy pi 可能接近所有这些板的 90-95%，因为它们都源自基础参考设计。 我们在设计硬件时关注的重点是选择供应链中断较少的组件。组件被替换（例如，我们牺牲了 BT 5.0，因为它仍将承受压力），替换为更可能不会经历中断的组件。 该板的设计目标是长期可用。

在软件方面，我们也坚持我们的目标，即简单、稳定和企业级。 此外，该团队还提供了使用基于 Yocto 的发行版（Shunya）的选项，该发行版是定制的、简约的，并且可以在原型必须进入批量生产并变得更高效时根据应用程序需求进行精确定制。

我们是唯一一个专注于软件的团队，我们不止于提供兼容的操作系统或移植 raspberry pi 库，我们提供 AI 和 IoT 应用程序样板，可以用作构建现实生活解决方案的基础。

与其他地区供应商的市场明智比较：

  • 欧洲供应商：优质的电路板但价格更高的支持
  • 美国供应商：优质板但难以获得支持，例如高通高价不向所有人公开芯片设计，数量低于 10k。
  • 台湾：例如 Asus Tinkerboard 2，中等价格但几乎不支持仍然提供较旧的内核 4.4，支持有限（主要是 android）
  • 所有中国供应商：相互抄袭，硬件和软件稳定性低中国策略主要是以更低的价格发布具有越来越多具有吸引力的纸质功能的板卡，而不是花时间稳定软件并提供对发布功能稳定性的问责制。 策略：提供一个稳定的电路板，在发现错误时不断修复错误并为客户维护它，这样他们就可以轻松地设计产品，而不必处理意外情况。 是的，我们来自印度🇮🇳。印度人从西方学习了流程，现在以更低的价格以相同的标准实施。 创始人也在中国生活了8年⇨🇳，非常了解硬件设计和供应链。 所以在某种程度上，这是硬件和软件两个世界中最好的。

你为什么不能大量生产它并让所有人都可以购买？

两个原因：

  • 如果我们不是一家自力更生的初创公司，我们会做大量生产并提供给所有人的产品。因此，如果您下大订单，可用性会通过更好的设计来解决，但阻止我们的只是资金，而不是筹码。

  • 只是短期销售意味着支持原型设计者、实验者和客户的各种用例，他们会尝试随机的东西，支持体验会受到影响。
    我们的关键区别在于我们是为认真的人服务的，我们将支持该产品，而这会损害它。所以我们选择选择客户，慢慢来，但很好地支持他们。

当然 ！！ 总有一个折衷。 我们专注于服务企业市场，我们的目标是提供稳定的产品，而不是不断追逐新的但不稳定的功能带来的兴奋。 这就是为什么您不会看到很多具有我们更新配置的消费级主板。 还有其他人比我们更好地服务于该市场。 我们的重点是企业的长期稳定性，因此您不必不断地重新设计。 我们就像高端品牌手机，功能总是很晚，但非常稳定。

对黑客们说声抱歉，我们知道你们想要新板的所有乐趣，但我们有点倾向于商业稳定的产品。

简短回答：谷歌、工业和 AI 变体可用于同一 SoC


长答案请继续阅读
选择 RK3399 是出于相同的企业原因，而不是其他速度更快但尚未得到证实的 SoC。 它以非常高的频率更新。我们从Google 的内核回购中学习并获得了很多对我们内核的修复。 与飞思卡尔或 TI 或高通不同，中国的 SoC BSP 可以正常工作，但还有很大的改进空间。像谷歌这样的客户在软件层面做得更多。 因此，我们知道消费级 SoC 的 RK3566/68 等将存在许多未知问题，并且在大量部署或像 Apple 或 Google 这样的专家公司检查所有这些问题之前可能仍然未知。 此外，这些消费级 SoC 的使用寿命较短，因此最终当它变得稳定时，SoC 可能会供不应求。 请放心，我们为客户提供物超所值、更便宜*但不妥协的产品。 此外，RK3399 在工业级温度版本和 RK3399 pro（AI 版本）的另外两个变体中提供引脚对引脚兼容，因此该设计将长期保留以满足您不断增长的应用需求，这就是我们将重点放在这一方面的方式。

我们从 2017 年开始（作为Shunyaos和IoTIoT团队）。硬件设计于 2021 年 12 月开始。目前，在测试版中，我们专注于两件事，让电路板在软件方面非常稳定，我们的早期访问客户和测试人员报告未知错误，我们会及时解决它们。 在下一阶段，我们计划将其变成一个随时可用的平台（想想现成的披萨面包），客户只需在上面添加他们的附加物（想想配料）、编写代码（想想烘焙）并在市场上进行验证。 如果他们用他们的原型打出全垒打，我们总是在那里帮助他们把它提升到一个新的水平。

是的，披萨面包的东西会很酷，期待大量的 AI 和 IoT 项目以及其他有用的现成产品代码很快会在网站上发布。 请查看我们的文档页面并在linkedin上关注我们


在这里查看。

一旦被暂停，brainypi 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

解除暂停后，brainypi 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，brainypi 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 brainypi 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 brainypi 的方法：

降低 brainypi 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

不屈不挠的 brainypi 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Brainy Pi is a single board computer made by entrepreneurs for entrepreneurs and businesses .
This blog will tell you why brainy pi exists ? The unsolved problem, the innovative approach , why us , comparision with peers and the future of Brainy pi. You will know it all at the end of it.

Why Brainy pi exists?
The Unsolved Problem: A lot of business ideas can do better with customised hardware .Compared to software hardware design still remains hard.Building custom software by integrating different modules has become common and easy which has brought so many niche software solutions .Can we get this modularity available in Software to hardware design and help push more next generation AI and IoT products faster into market? Raspberry pi and Arduino showed the world atleast faster prototypes are now surely possible .
But from Prototype to Mass Production it still remains a hard design journey and many kickstarter products never get delivered . So many creative ideas lost for humanity.

Image source : qualityinspection.org

Solution : Can we get the best of 2 worlds ? Speed while prototyping , Scalable design for Manufacturing ?
Brainy pi was created with this exact thing in mind. Brainy Pi builds further on the once great idea of Raspberry pi made primarily for prototypers.

  • The new approach: Brainy Pi is a full hardware and software solution that bridges the gap between prototyping and mass production. It leverages the software ecosystem of Raspberry pi for fast prototyping, but is customizable for mass production.

How about the code ? Wont we have to re do all the code if hardware is customised or changed?

The magic : Brainy pi backed by Shunya os team can ensure all code written for Brainy pi / Raspberry pi can be ported smoothly into any Arm(Cortex -A) based hardware opening a wide range of SoC(chip) choices in terms of configuration and pricing for their final product.

Q1) Really!! or is Brainy Pi just another clone of Raspberry Pi ?What is different ? Every other clone of Raspberry pi is mostly a hardware clone trying to make a quick buck on the demand generated by Raspberry pi . Brainy pi is not a clone but inspired by what they did .Brainy pi is a full hardware and software solution unlike others we don't plan to release too many boards .

Brainy pi will provide enterprise grade stability and support the software stack to keep it bug free , so people can easily build quality solutions on top of it .

Q2) Tell me more , a purpose , a goal that is different from other boards? In one line : Stable , Reliable , Long term availability , well documented and supported , focussed on serious product developers and entrepreneurs . Brainy pi has a clear reason to exist : Create an easy path for entrepreneurs/enterprises to go from existing/new prototypes to mass production . Brainy pi is designed with this in mind in both software and hardware.

The purpose is not to launch and sell a plethora of boards but to make a stable reliable board and maintain it long term as a platform of choice to build great products on .

Q3) I see many similar boards from Europe ,Taiwan and Chinese vendors? Can you compare a little more ?
The core chip RK3399 is used by many from Google Chromebook , Asus Tinkerboard 2, many Chinese vendors Orange , Nano , Rock and other pi ’s . All of them use Rockchip’s tablet pc reference design as the base and then modify as per their goals . So in hardware Brainy pi maybe anywhere between 90–95% close to all these boards since they all originate from the base reference design . What we have focussed while designing the hardware is choosing components which will have low supply chain disruption .Components were substituted (e.g we sacrificed BT 5.0 since it will remain under pressure ) with ones which are more likely to not go through disruption . The board is designed with a purpose of being long term available .

In software too, we stick to our purpose, being easy yet stable and enterprise grade . Rbian(Raspbian compatible debian) is created with the idea of being compatible with Raspberry pi code .One of the key goals is that all Raspberry pi code should directly run on Brainy pi for ease of use still we use only LTS Linux kernels for reliability of system.

Further the team offers an option to use Yocto based distro(Shunya) which is custom , minimalistic and can be tailored exactly for the application needs when the prototype has to go into mass production and become more efficient .

We are the only team which has deep focus on software we dont stop at providing a compatible OS or porting raspberry pi libraries we offer AI and IoT application boiler plates , that can be used as a base to build real life solutions .

A Market wise comparision with vendors from other region :

  • Europe Vendors: Quality boards but higher priced support
  • US vendors : Quality board but hard to access support e.g Qualcomm high price not open to reveal chip design to everyone with sub 10k volume.
  • Taiwan : E.g Asus Tinkerboard 2 , Medium price but almost no support still offers older kernel 4.4 with limited support (mostly only android)
  • All Chinese vendors : Close copies of each other with low stability in both hardware and software Chinese Strategy is largely to release boards with more and more attractive paper features at lower prices , but not bother to stabilise software and offer accountability on released feature stability. Brainy Pi : Low price with good support . Strategy : Offer one stable board keep fixing bugs as they get discovered and maintain it for customers so they have an easy time designing products and dont have to deal with surprises . And yes , we come from India 🇮🇳 .Indians have learnt processes from west and now implements it at same standards at much lower price . The founder also lived in China 🇨🇳 for 8 years and understands hardware design and its supply chain very well . So in a way this is best of 2 worlds in hardware and software.

Q4)You said the hardware is designed to improve availability yet it is not available for instant purchase why do I have to apply ? Why cant you mass produce it and make it available for buying for all ?

Two reasons :

  • 1) Funding : We had budget just enough to produce a minimum batch of 3000 (minimum the factory could accept for SMT ) By giving these 3000 pcs to the right customers(influencers and volume buyers) we need to find enough orders to keep producing atleast 5000 pcs every month. If we weren’t a bootstrapped startup we would do exactly that produce a lot and make it available to all .So if you place a large orders availability is solved by better design ,but it is only the money that stops us not the chips.

  • 2) Quality support over quantity sales : We could have used the initial excitement made a quick buck selling all 3k pieces to just anyone who wanted to buy .We are in this for long term . Just short term selling would mean supporting all kinds of uses cases by prototypers , experimenters and customers who would try random things and support experience would suffer .
    Our key differentiation is we are for serious folks and we will support the product and this would have compromised it .So we choose to select customers ,go slow but support them well .

Q5) Everything has a downside , What are the downsides of slow but stable approach ? Sure !! There is always a trade off . We are focused on serving the enterprise market, and our goal is to provide a stable product rather than constantly chasing the excitement of new but unstable features. That’s why you won’t see a lot of consumer-grade boards with the newer configurations from us. There are others who serve that market better than us. Our focus is on long-term stability for enterprises, so you don’t have to keep redesigning. . We are like the premium brand phone that always gets its features late but is very stable .

Sorry to the hackers we know you want all the fun with new boards but we lean a bit towards commercially stable products.

Q6) If it is about long term why not choose a newer SoC from Rockchip like Rk3566/68 etc ?
Short answer : Google ,Industrial and AI variant available for same SoC


Google Logo (no association with Brainy pi)

For long answer read on
Choosing RK3399 was for the same enterprise reasons, against other more faster SoCs which are yet to be proven. Rk3999 is used in Google Chromebook and Google continues to work on its kernel since 2016 . It is updated at a very high frequency .We learnt and got a lot of fixes into our kernel from Google’s kernel repo . Unlike Freescale or TI or Qualcomm Chinese SoCs BSPs just work but leave a lot of room for improvement .A customer like Google does lot more at software level. So we know there will be many unknown issues with the consumer grade SoC’s RK3566/68 etc and may remain unknown until a lot of it is deployed or an expert Company like Apple or Google looks through all of it . Besides these consumer grade SoCs will have shorter lifetime so eventually by the time it gets stable the SoC may be out of supply. Stability over excitement in designs is what Brainy pi offers to its customers. Brainy pi founder was behind launch of many consumer products and has insider view of issues in SoCs which are not known to a new designer . Rest assured we offer something that is a great value to customer , cheaper* but not compromised. *Compared to Asus Tinkerboard 2 ($179 list price) and Google Chromebook which uses this SoC we have kept our prices ex-factory in the range of 75$ .

Also , RK3399 is available pin to pin compatbile in two more variants Industrial grade temperature version and RK3399 pro (AI version) so the design will remain for long term for your growing application needs thats how we zeroed down on this one.

Q7) What are the future plans with Brainy pi?
We been on this since 2017 (as Shunyaos and IoTIoT team ).Hardware design started in Dec 2021. Currently while in beta we are focussed on 2 things, getting the board very stable in terms of software ,our early acess customers and testers report unknown bugs and we solve them promptly as we know it . As next stage we plan to make it into a ready to use platform (think a ready made pizza bread ) on which customers just have to put their add ons(think toppings) , write code (think baking it) and validate in market . If they hit a home run with their prototype , we are always there to help them take it to next level .

And yes , the pizza bread thing is going to be way cool , expect a lot of AI and IoT projects and other useful ready product codes to be released soon on the website . Please check our documentation page and follow us on linkedin


Ps: There is an youtube channel which has many live projects with brainy pi. Check it out here.

item.title: 在 Brainy Pi 上使用 OpenCV 进行目标检测 | Object detection using OpenCV on Brainy Pi
item.description:
目标检测是计算机视觉的一个令人兴奋的领域，具有许多实际应用。 从自动驾驶汽车到安全系统，物体检测可以帮助机器理解周围的世界。 近年来，基于深度学习的方法在目标检测任务中实现最先进的性能方面显示出巨大的希望。 然而，由于复杂的模型和硬件要求，实施这些方法可能具有挑战性。 幸运的是，随着 Brainy Pi 等单板计算机的出现，现在可以使用OpenCV等开源库部署复杂的对象检测模型。 在这篇博文中，我们将探讨如何在Brainy Pi上使用 OpenCV 执行对象检测，并提供分步指南以开始使用这项激动人心的技术。
概述

在深入研究代码之前，让我们首先讨论项目结构以及使用的不同文件和文件夹objectDetection.py文件包含使用单次检测器 (SSD) 算法以 MobileNetV3 作为基础网络进行对象检测的 Python 代码。 该代码使用 OpenCV 库进行图像和视频处理。requirements.txt 文件包含运行对象检测代码所需的 Python 库列表。 让我们使用以下命令安装库。

 pip install -r requirements.txt

单发检测器 (SSD) 算法

它是一种单级检测器，可直接预测图像中所有对象的边界框和类标签，而无需使用单独的区域建议网络。 这使得它比其他对象检测算法更快、更高效。SSD 算法使用卷积神经网络 (CNN) 从输入图像中提取特征，然后应用一组检测头来预测每个对象的类标签和边界框目的。 使用 OpenCV 运行目标检测

要使用 OpenCV 执行对象检测，我们首先需要安装必要的库。 我们可以通过在终端中运行以下命令来做到这一点：

 git clone https://github.com/brainypi/brainypi-opencv-examples.git cd object-detection pip install -r requirements.txt

安装库后，我们可以通过运行以下命令来运行对象检测代码：

 python objectDetection.py

这将启动对象检测脚本，该脚本从相机捕获帧并使用 SSD 模型检测帧内的对象。 然后，检测到的对象会实时显示在屏幕上，周围带有边框。要退出程序，请按 q 键。
调整代码供您自己使用

如果您想将此人脸识别代码用于您自己的项目，则需要进行一些更改以针对您的特定用例对其进行自定义。 以下是调整代码的一些技巧：

 `detectObjects()` – Function which is responsible for detection objects - Inputs: - img ([cv2.Mat]): Input Image - thres ([int]): Object detection threshold - draw (bool, optional): Draw binding boxes in output frame. Defaults to True. - objects (list, optional): List of objects to filter, ie will only detect these objects. Defaults to []. - Outputs: - img [cv2.Mat]: Output image - objectInfo [list]: Object information

我们可以按如下方式使用此功能

threshold = 0.45 result, objectInfo = detectObjects(img, threshold, 0.2)

让我们更改对象检测阈值以消除误报。

 threshold = 0.85 result, objectInfo = detectObjects(img, threshold, 0.2)

现在它只会检测模型对对象有 85% 把握的对象。 假设您只想检测 1 或 2 个物体，例如椅子或书籍，那么您可以

threshold = 0.85 objects = ["chair", "books"] result, objectInfo = detectObjects(img, threshold, 0.2, true, objects)

所以只会检测椅子和书籍并过滤掉所有其他对象。以下示例用于手机和人员检测。

您可以在此处查看代码中的完整示例 — github.com/brainypi/brainypi-opencv-exa ...

结论

在 brainypi 上使用 OpenCV 进行对象检测可用于许多 Edge 应用程序。 在本教程中，我们探讨了如何使用 Python、OpenCV 和 SSD 算法执行实时对象检测。 我们还讨论了对代码的一些改进。 有了这些知识，您就可以在 Brainy Pi 上开发自己的物体检测系统。 查看更多 opencv 应用程序

一旦被暂停，brainypi 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

解除暂停后，brainypi 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，brainypi 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 brainypi 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 brainypi 的方法：

降低 brainypi 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

不屈不挠的 brainypi 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Object detection is an exciting area of computer vision that has numerous practical applications. From self-driving cars to security systems, object detection can help machines make sense of the world around them. In recent years, deep learning-based approaches have shown great promise in achieving state-of-the-art performance in object detection tasks. However, implementing these approaches can be challenging due to the complex models and hardware requirements. Fortunately, with the advent of single-board computers like the Brainy Pi, it is now possible to deploy sophisticated object detection models using open-source libraries like OpenCV. In this blog post, we will explore how to perform object detection using OpenCV on the Brainy Pi, and provide a step-by-step guide to get started with this exciting technology.
Overview

Before diving into the code, let’s first discuss the project structure and the different files and folders used.The objectDetection.py file contains the Python code for object detection using the Single Shot Detector (SSD) algorithm with MobileNetV3 as the base network. The code uses the OpenCV library for image and video processing.The requirements.txt file contains a list of the Python libraries required to run the object detection code. Let’s install libraries using the following command.

pip install -r requirements.txt

The Single Shot Detector (SSD) Algorithm

The Single Shot Detector (SSD) algorithm is a popular deep learning-based approach for object detection. It is a one-stage detector that directly predicts the bounding boxes and class labels for all objects in an image, without using a separate region proposal network. This makes it faster and more efficient than other object detection algorithms.The SSD algorithm uses a convolutional neural network (CNN) to extract features from the input image, and then applies a set of detection heads to predict the class labels and bounding boxes for each object. The MobileNetV3 architecture is a popular choice for the base network in SSD, as it is lightweight and optimized for mobile devices.

Running Object Detection with OpenCV

To perform object detection with OpenCV, we first need to install the necessary libraries. We can do this by running the following commands in the terminal:

git clone https://github.com/brainypi/brainypi-opencv-examples.git
cd object-detection
pip install -r requirements.txt

Once the libraries are installed, we can run the object detection code by running the following command:

python objectDetection.py

This will start the object detection script, which captures frames from the camera and uses the SSD model to detect objects within the frames. The detected objects are then displayed on the screen in real-time, with bounding boxes around them.To exit the program, press the q key.
Adapting the Code for Your Own Use

If you want to use this face recognition code for your own project, you’ll need to make some changes to customize it for your specific use case. Here are some tips for adapting the code:

`detectObjects()` – Function which is responsible for detection objects

- Inputs:
  - img ([cv2.Mat]): Input Image
  - thres ([int]): Object detection threshold
  - draw (bool, optional): Draw binding boxes in output frame. Defaults to True.
  - objects (list, optional): List of objects to filter, i.e will only detect these objects. Defaults to [].

- Outputs:
  - img [cv2.Mat]: Output image
  - objectInfo [list]: Object information

We can use this function as follow

threshold = 0.45 result, objectInfo = detectObjects(img, threshold, 0.2)

Let’s change the object detection threshold to remove false positives.

threshold = 0.85 result, objectInfo = detectObjects(img, threshold, 0.2)

Now it will only detect objects which the model is 85% sure of the object. Let’s say you want to detect only 1 or 2 objects like chair or books, then you can

threshold = 0.85 objects = ["chair", "books"] result, objectInfo = detectObjects(img, threshold, 0.2, true, objects)

so will only detect chair and books and filter out all the other objects.Following example is for Cell phone and Person detection.

You can see the full example in the code here — github.com/brainypi/brainypi-opencv-exa…

Conclusion

Object detection using OpenCV on brainypi can be used for many Edge applications. In this tutorial, we explored how to perform real-time object detection using Python, OpenCV, and the SSD algorithm. We also discussed some improvements to the code. With this knowledge, you can develop your own object detection system on Brainy Pi. Checkout more opencv applications

Ecaterina Teodoroiu - Apr 7

item.title: 为什么在网站中使用 HTML？ | WHY USE HTML IN A WEBSITES ?
item.description:
辛格

发表于 4 月 7 日

它提供了一种描述网页结构和呈现方式的方法，包括文本、图像、链接和其他媒体。

结构：HTML 提供了一种将 Web 内容组织成逻辑部分的方法，例如标题、段落、列表和表格。 这使搜索引擎更容易索引和理解页面内容。

可访问性：HTML 为创建可供残障人士使用的可访问网站提供了基础。 通过使用语义 HTML，开发人员可以确保内容被正确标记和组织，使屏幕阅读器和其他辅助技术更容易解读页面。

调整：HTML 为创建网页提供了一套标准的元素和属性。 这确保页面在不同的浏览器和设备上保持一致，并且可以随着时间的推移轻松维护和更新。

通过使用语义 HTML 并优化页面标题、描述和其他元数据，开发人员可以提高其站点在搜索引擎结果中的可见性。

协调：HTML 可以与其他 Web 技术集成，例如用于样式的 CSS 和用于交互的 JavaScript。 这允许开发人员创建丰富的、动态的 Web 应用程序，这些应用程序可以提供更具吸引力的用户体验。

原文:

Prithwi singh

Posted on Apr 7

HTML (Hypertext Markup Language) is the standard markup language used to create and structure content on the web. It provides a way to describe the structure and presentation of web pages, including text, images, links, and other media.

Structure: HTML provides a way to structure web content into logical sections, such as headings, paragraphs, lists, and tables. This makes it easier for search engines to index and understand the content of the page.

Accessibility: HTML provides the foundation for creating accessible websites that can be used by people with disabilities. By using semantic HTML, developers can ensure that the content is properly labeled and organized, making it easier for screen readers and other assistive technologies to interpret the page.

Adjustment: HTML provides a standard set of elements and attributes for creating web pages. This ensures that pages are consistent across different browsers and devices, and that they can be easily maintained and updated over time.

SEO: HTML provides the structure and metadata that search engines use to index web pages. By using semantic HTML and optimizing the page title, description, and other metadata, developers can improve the visibility of their site in search engine results.

Co-ordination: HTML can be integrated with other web technologies, such as that CSS for styling and JavaScript for interactivity. This allows developers to create rich, dynamic web applications that can more engaging user experience.

item.title: 回收码：CNAME & 重新请求 | Recycle Code: CNAME & Re-request
item.description:
本周，我在Starchart项目上做了一些工作，我重用了现有代码来完成我的任务。 当我发现如何利用现有代码来完成任务时，这总是一种美妙的感觉。

没有重复的 CNAME！

在一个证书下，用户可以拥有多个DNS Records 。
按照设计，CNAME DNS 记录会将您从一个域转发到另一个域，因此让两个 CNAME DNS 记录同时将您从一个域转发到另外两个域毫无意义。

因此，我们需要防止用户创建此类重复的 CNAME 记录。

一个小限制

我添加了一段代码来实现这一点：

 if (type === 'CNAME') { count = await prisma.dnsRecord.count({ where: { username, type, subdomain, }, });

该项目已经有一些代码来检查名称、类型和值相同的重复 DNS 记录。 名称是子域名。 值可能是 IP 地址或域之类的东西，具体取决于 DNS 记录的类型。

我添加的这段代码位于现有代码中，用于检查重复的 DNS 记录，以更好地适应项目并重新利用其他现有代码。 通过计算属于同一用户和具有相同子域的 CNAME 记录的数量，这增加了检查重复 DNS 记录的另一个方法。 选择这两个条件是因为用户名和子域名用于创建 CNAME 记录将从中重定向用户的域名。
回想一下，我们不想从一个域重定向到多个域。 这就是我们检查具有相同用户和子域的 CNAME 记录的原因。

它利用 Prisma 的count在数据库中进行计数。 重新申请证书

说到证书，我还和队友一起做了一个PR和peer programming ，以增加用户在即将到期时重新申请新证书的能力。

我们做了什么

该代码检查证书的validTo日期是否距现在不到 30 天。 如果是这样，重新请求按钮将在证书页面上可用，以允许用户请求另一个证书。
因为我们已经有了允许用户请求他们的第一个证书的现有代码，所以我们只需为按钮再次使用/触发该代码。

按钮的代码片段：

 <Flex justifyContent="flex-end"> <Form method="post" onSubmit={() => setIsDisabled(true)}> <IconButton type="submit" aria-label="renew-certificate" icon={<RepeatIcon />} backgroundColor="transparent" color="black" _hover={{ backgroundColor: 'brand.500', color: 'white' }} isDisabled={isDisabled} /> </Form> </Flex>

因为证书请求代码位于使用remix.js构建的操作函数下，并且它是由提交触发的，所以我们只需让这个新按钮提交以重用该代码。

我们确实发现了一个小错误，该程序只识别用户的第二个最新证书，因为代码按validTo日期降序对证书进行排序，以获取最新的证书，但真正的新证书不会有该字段短期内填写。 这导致新证书被放在排序的底部，而不是被选为当前证书。

对等编程

与另一个开发人员坐在一起编写代码很有趣。 您可以获得两种不同的观点，并且可以互相学习，无论是工具和语言方面的专业知识、编码风格还是新想法。

因为我不熟悉项目的前端，所以与熟悉该领域的另一位开发人员坐在一起非常有帮助。
他向我介绍了我们想要处理的前端代码区域是如何构建的，我们将我们的想法放在一起找出解决问题的方法。



原文:

This week, I did some work on the Starchart project where I reuse existing codes to accomplish my tasks. It's always a neat feeling when I find out how I can leverage existing code to achieve a task.

No Duplicate CNAME!

The Starchart project allows user to easily create SSL certificate by taking care of a lot of required steps. Under a certificate, users can have multiple DNS Records.
By design, CNAME DNS Records forward you from one domain to another, so it makes little sense to have two CNAME DNS Records forwarding you from one same domain to two other domains at the same time.

Because of this, we need to prevent users from creating these kind of duplicate CNAME records.

A Small Restriction

I added a piece of code to achieve that:

  if (type === 'CNAME') {
    count = await prisma.dnsRecord.count({
      where: {
        username,
        type,
        subdomain,
      },
    });

The project already has some code to check for duplicate DNS record that are the same with the name, type, and value. Name is the subdomain name. Type is the type of record, such as CNAME. Value would be things like IP address or domain, depending on the type of DNS record.

This piece of code I added sits in the existing code to check for duplicate DNS record, to fit the project better and reutilize other existing code. This adds another to checking for duplicate DNS record, by counting number of CNAME records that belong to the same user and has same subdomain. These two criteria are chosen because username and subdomain name are used to create the domain name where a CNAME record would redirect people from.
Recall that we don't want to redirect from one domain to multiple domain. This is why we check for CNAME records with same user and subdomain.

It utilizes Prisma's count to do the counting in the database. where specifies what criteria for a row to count.

Re-request Certificate

Speaking of certificate, I also did a PR with a teammate together with peer programming, to add the ability for users to re-request a new certificate when it's about to expire.

What we did

The code checks for whether a certificate's validTo date is less than 30 days from present. If so, a re-request button will become available on the certificate page to allow the user to request another certificate.
Because we already have existing code to allow users to request their first certificate, we simply use/trigger that code again for the button.

Code Snippet for the button:

              <Flex justifyContent="flex-end">
                <Form method="post" onSubmit={() => setIsDisabled(true)}>
                  <IconButton
                    type="submit"
                    aria-label="renew-certificate"
                    icon={<RepeatIcon />}
                    backgroundColor="transparent"
                    color="black"
                    _hover={{ backgroundColor: 'brand.500', color: 'white' }}
                    isDisabled={isDisabled}
                  />
                </Form>
              </Flex>

Because the certificate request code is under an action function build with remix.js, and it's triggered by submit, we simply make this new button submit to reuse that code.

We did found out a minor bug where the program was only recognizing the second newest certificate for a user, because the code was sorting certificates by validTo date in descending order, to fetch the newest one, but a really new one would not have that field filled out for a short period. This caused the new certificate to be placed at the bottom of the sorting and not be select as the current certificate.

Peer programming

It's interesting sitting with another developer to work on code together. You get tow different perspectives, and you can learn form each other, whether it's expertise with tools and languages, coding style, or new ideas.

Because I'm unfamiliar with the front-end of the project, it was very helpful to sit with another developer who is familiar with that area.
He walked me through how the area of the front-end code we wanted to work on is constructed, and we put our ideas together to figure out how to tackle the issue.
